command:
echo "=== HOP 3: Does cloudflared tunnel (.191) log the sakura request when sent? ==="
# Enable info-level request logging visibility: watch cloudflared journal during a send
ssh -i /home/jose/.ssh/n8n_bridge -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 jose@192.168.0.202 \
  'curl -s -X POST "https://api.telegram.org/bot8849049212:AAH2CHM_CDLDeTLfVxw7UGz5aO1weVBdnpw/sendMessage" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"chat_id\":\"-1003934415467\",\"text\":\"TUNNEL LOG TEST 0xFEED\"}" >/dev/null; echo sent'
sleep 8

echo "=== cloudflared .191 journal (any sakura/8080/request activity in last min)? ==="
sudo journalctl -u cloudflared --since "1 minute ago" --no-pager 2>/dev/null | grep -iE "sakura|8080|202|request|POST|error|err" | tail -15
echo "(empty = request never reached the tunnel = died at Cloudflare edge)"

echo ""
echo "=== Compare: GET to sakura works? (proves tunnel CAN reach .202 for GET) ==="
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "GET sakura: %{http_code}\n" --max-time 10 https://sakura.cutegrafiks.com/ 2>/dev/null
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "POST sakura/telegram (no secret): %{http_code}\n" -X POST --max-time 10 https://sakura.cutegrafiks.com/telegram -d '{}' 2>/dev/null

echo ""
echo "=== KEY TEST: does a POST with CORRECT secret via PUBLIC url reach .202? ==="
# This is the exact same request Telegram makes
ssh -i /home/jose/.ssh/n8n_bridge -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 jose@192.168.0.202 bash << 'REMOTE'
LB=$(wc -l < /home/jose/.hermes/logs/gateway.log)
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "  public POST w/secret: %{http_code}\n" \
  -X POST https://sakura.cutegrafiks.com/telegram \
  -H "X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token: 65b0642ca8a7fc2ae28fa8e8234895cd38fc88933dd9bf6a45157546c66b98a6" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"update_id":55555,"message":{"message_id":55555,"date":1718000000,"chat":{"id":-1003934415467,"type":"supergroup"},"from":{"id":8869156588,"is_bot":false,"first_name":"Jose"},"text":"PUBLIC POST 0xCAFE"}}' --max-time 12 2>/dev/null
sleep 8
echo "  gateway.log: $(tail -n +$((LB+1)) /home/jose/.hermes/logs/gateway.log 2>/dev/null | grep -c inbound) new inbound msgs"
tail -n +$((LB+1)) /home/jose/.hermes/logs/gateway.log 2>/dev/null | grep inbound | tail -3
REMOTE
